ddls:
CREATE TABLE "posts" (
  "post_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each post */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the post */,
  "score" INTEGER /* Score or engagement metric of the post */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who created the post */,
  "created_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp when the post was created */,
  "full_link" TEXT /* Full link to the post */,
  "num_comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the post */,
  "over_18" INTEGER /* Whether the post is over 18 */,
  "date_stamp" TEXT /* Date stamp of the post */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* ID of the social media platform */,
  "post_type" TEXT /* Type of the post (e.g., text, image, video) */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the post belongs to */,
  "engagement_score" INTEGER /* Combined engagement score */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views the post has received */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of times the post has been shared */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the post has received */,
  "dislikes" INTEGER /* Number of dislikes the post has received */,
  "last_modified_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of the last modification */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the post */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the post is deleted */,
  "deleted_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who deleted the post */,
  "deleted_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of the deletion */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the post (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_platform_id FOREIGN KEY ("platform_id") REFERENCES platforms ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Name of the author */,
  "author_flair_text" TEXT /* Flair text or badge of the author */,
  "removed_by" TEXT /* Who removed the author (if any) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Bio or description of the author */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL to the author's profile picture */,
  "follower_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the author has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the author is following */,
  "join_date_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of when the author joined the platform */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the author is verified */,
  "last_active_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of the last activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awards" (
  "award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each award */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* ID of the post that received the award */,
  "award_type" TEXT /* Type of award (e.g., gold, silver, bronze) */,
  "awarded_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of when the award was given */,
  "awarded_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who awarded */,
  "award_description" TEXT /* Description of the award */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("award_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_awards_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awarders" (
  "awarder_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each awarder */,
  "award_id" INTEGER /* ID of the award given by the awarder */,
  "awarder_name" TEXT /* Name of the awarder */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the awarder */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Bio or description of the awarder */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL to the awarder's profile picture */,
  "follower_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the awarder has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the awarder is following */,
  "join_date_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of when the awarder joined the platform */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the awarder is verified */,
  "last_active_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of the last activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("awarder_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_awarders_award_id FOREIGN KEY ("award_id") REFERENCES awards ("award_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, moderator) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Bio or description of the user */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile picture */,
  "join_date_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of when the user joined the platform */,
  "last_active_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of the last activity */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON or text field to store user permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "platforms" (
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each platform */,
  "platform_name" TEXT /* Name of the platform */,
  "platform_url" TEXT /* URL of the platform */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key for accessing the platform */,
  "api_secret" TEXT /* API secret for accessing the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_modifications" (
  "modification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each modification */,
  "post_id" TEXT /* ID of the post that was modified */,
  "modified_utc" INTEGER /* Unix timestamp of the modification */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the modification */,
  "modification_type" TEXT /* Type of modification (e.g., edit, delete) */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value before the modification */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value after the modification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("modification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_post_modifications_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_post_modifications_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)
